mechanism: Credit impulse leads equity cycle turns by 2-6 months through lending conditions affecting corporate investment and earnings. The credit impulse deceleration transmits through business investment, inventory, and consumption channels to corporate profits.
regime_dependence: Strongest when credit is the binding constraint. May be attenuated when fiscal deficits provide an alternative profit channel (Kalecki). Less informative during QE periods when credit supply is artificially unconstrained.
